en: Why are you late? You're not going to like the answer. I already know the answer.
I missed the bus. I don't doubt it. No bus stops near Brad's.
ko: 대답은 너가 싫어할 거야. 이미 답을 알아.

en: You spent the night, the alarm didn't work, or maybe it did.
I didn't sleep with him. Girl, I mean, there's something... I missed the bus!
ko: 브래드 집 근처에 버스 정류장이 있어.

en: There's something either very wrong with you, or there's something very wrong with him.
ko: 너 밤새 있었지? 알람이 안 울렸거나, 아니면 울렸을 수도 있어.

en: There's nothing wrong with him. Please tell me you know that for a fact.
Mommy, I gotta go. You're lying, aren't you? I wouldn't lie to you.
ko: 엄마, 가야 해. 거짓말하는 거지? 거짓말 안 해.

en: Good morning, guys.
ja: おはようございます, guys.

en: Good morning, Miss Rebecca. Everybody's in their seats?
ko: 아침 인사해, 여러분. 안녕하세요, 레베카 선생님. 모두 자리에 앉아있네.
ja: おはようございます, Miss Rebecca. Everybody's in their seats?
zhTW: 早安, Miss Rebecca. Everybody's in their seats?
vi: 아침 인사해, 여러분. 안녕하세요, 레베카 선생님. 모두 자리에 앉아있네.

en: Yes. Okay.
Sydney, why don't you tell us what you did this weekend?
ko: 좋아, 시드니. 이번 주말에 뭐 했는지 말해줘.

en: Come on, Sydney, we know you're not shy.
How come you always have to tell me what we did and you don't tell us what you did?
ko: 너가 수줍어하지 않는 거 알아. 왜 항상 우리가 한 걸 말해야 하고 너는 말하지 않아?

en: Okay. I had a really great weekend,
but you can't tell Miss Melanie, okay? What did you do?
ko: 정말 멋진 주말을 보냈어. 하지만 멜라니 선생님에게는 말하지 마.

en: I made a new friend. It's so much fun to make new friends, isn't it?
ko: 새 친구를 사귀었어. 새로운 친구를 만드는 게 정말 재밌지?

en: Yeah. Did you tell your mom and dad about your new friend?
Absolutely. You should never keep anything from your parents.
ko: 너의 새 친구에 대해 부모님께 말했어?

en: And I told mine. Cluck, cluck. Cluck.
ko: 부모님께는 절대 비밀을 지키면 안 돼. 나도 내 부모님께 말했어.

en: Cluck. Hibakura. Cluck.
Cluck, cluck.
ko: 너의 새 친구에 대해 부모님께 말했어?

en: Hibakura.
C-A-L-H-E-R-A.
The. We know that word, the.
Oh.
29-year-old female, first seizure one month ago,
ko: 29세 여성, 한 달 전 첫 발작 후 말하는 능력을 잃고 어버버거렸다.

en: lost the ability to speak, babbled like a baby, aggressive deterioration of mental status.
ko: 말을 잃고 아기처럼 중얼거리고, 정신 상태가 공격적으로 악화됐어.

en: See that? They all assume that I'm a patient because of this cane.
So put on a white coat like the rest of us. I don't want them to think I'm a doctor.
ko: 그러면 우리처럼 흰 가운을 입어. 의사로 보이고 싶지 않아.

en: You see why the administration might have a problem with that attitude?
ko: 행정부가 그런 태도를 문제 삼을 이유가 보이지?

en: People don't want a sick doctor. That's fair enough. I don't like healthy patients.
The 29-year-old female. The one who can't talk, I like that part.
She's my cousin. And your cousin doesn't like the diagnosis.
I wouldn't either. Brain tumor. She's gonna die. Boring.
ko: 그 부분이 마음에 들어, 그녀는 내 사촌이야

en: No wonder you're such a renowned diagnostician. You don't need to actually know anything to figure out what's wrong.
You're the oncologist. I'm just a lowly infectious disease guy.
Yes, just a simple country doctor. Brain tumors at her age are highly unlikely.
She's 29. Whatever she's got is highly unlikely.
The protein markers for the three most prevalent brain cancers came out negative.
ko: 너가 그렇게 유명한 진단의사인 이유가 있네

en: It's an HMO lab. Might as well have sent it to a high school kid with a chemistry set.
ko: 뭘 아는지 상관없이 문제를 알아내는 거지

en: No family history. What's your uncle got of cancer?
ko: 너는 종양학자고, 나는 그냥 평범한 감염병 의사야

en: Other side. No environmental factors. That you know of.
And she's not responding to radiation treatment. None of which is even close to dispositive.
All it does is raise one question. Your cousin goes to an HMO?
ko: 29세야, 그녀가 뭘 앓고 있든 가능성이 낮아

en: Come on. Why leave all the fun for the car?
What's the point of putting together a team if you're not gonna use them?
You've got three overqualified doctors working for you.
Getting bored.
I'm bored.
It's a lesion.
And the big green thing in the middle of the bigger blue thing on a map is an island.
I was hoping for something a bit more creative.
Shouldn't we be speaking to the patient before we start diagnosing?
Is she a doctor? No, but everybody lies.
ko: 그녀가 의사야? 아니, 하지만 모두 거짓말을 해.

en: Dr. Haas doesn't like dealing with patients. Isn't treating patients why we became doctors?
ko: 하우스 박사는 환자 상대를 싫어해. 환자 치료하려고 의사가 된 거 아니야?

en: No, treating illnesses is why we became doctors.
Treating patients is what makes most doctors miserable.
So you're trying to eliminate the humanity from the practice of medicine.
If we don't talk to them, they can't lie to us. And we can't lie to them.
ko: 왜 차를 위해 모든 재미를 남겨두는 거야? 팀을 꾸리는 의미가 뭐야?

en: Humanity is overrated. I don't think it's a tumor.
ko: 인류는 과대평가돼. 난 그게 종양이라고 생각하지 않아.

en: First year medical school. If you hear her feet, you'd think horses, not zebras.
Are you in first year medical school? No.
First of all, there's nothing on the CAT scan.
Second of all, if this is a horse, then her kindly family doctor in Trenton makes the obvious diagnosis
ko: 둘째, 이게 말이라면 트렌턴의 친절한 가족 의사가 명백한 진단을 내릴 거야.

en: and it never gets near this office. Differential diagnosis, people.
ko: 그리고 이 사무실에는 절대 오지 않아.

en: If it's not a tumor, what are the suspects? Why couldn't she talk?
ko: 감별 진단이야. 종양이 아니라면 의심할 만한 건 뭐지? 왜 말을 못해?

en: Aneurysm, stroke, or some other ischemic syndrome? Get her a contrast MRI.
ko: 동맥류, 뇌졸중, 아니면 다른 허혈 증후군이야.

en: Crutchfield-Jacob disease? Mad cow. Mad zebra.
Wernicke's encephalopathy. No. Blood thymine level was normal.
ko: 크러치필드-제이콥병, 광우병, 미친 얼룩말, 베르니케 뇌병증.

en: Lab in Trenton could have screwed up the blood test.
I assume it's a corollary of people lie that people screw up.
ko: 트렌턴의 실험실이 혈액 검사를 잘못했을 수도 있어.

en: Redraw the blood tests. And get her scheduled for that contrast MRI ASAP.
ko: 혈액 검사 다시 하고 대조 MRI를 최대한 빨리 예약해.

en: Let's find out what kind of zebra we're treating here.
Radiology, please call the page. Radiology, please call the page.
ko: 혈액 검사를 다시 하고 대조 MRI를 ASAP로 예약해. 우리가 어떤 얼룩말을 치료하고 있는지 알아보자.

en: Respiratory therapy. I was expecting you in my office 20 minutes ago.
ko: 방사선과, 페이지를 불러주세요. 방사선과, 페이지를 불러주세요.

en: Really? Well, that's odd, because I had no intention of being in your office 20 minutes ago.
You think we have nothing to talk about? No, I just can't think of anything I'd be interested in.
I signed your paychecks. I have tenure.
ko: 정말? 그건 이상하네, 왜냐하면 20분 전에 네 사무실에 있을 생각이 없었거든.

en: Are you going to grab my cane now and stop me from leaving? That would be juvenile.
ko: 우리가 이야기할 게 없다고 생각해? 아니, 그냥 내가 관심 있는 게 생각나지 않아.

en: I can still fire you if you're not doing your job. I'm here from 9 to 5.
Your billing is practically non-existent. Rough year.
You ignore requests for consults. I call back. Sometimes I miss the aisle.
ko: 상담 요청을 무시해. 전화해. 가끔 놓칠 때도 있지.

en: You're six years behind on your obligations to this clinic.
See, I was right. This doesn't interest me.
ko: 봐, 내가 맞았어. 이건 흥미롭지 않아.

en: Six years times three weeks. You owe me better than four months.
ko: 네가 일을 제대로 하지 않으면 여전히 해고할 수 있어.

en: It's 5 o'clock. I'm going home.
To what? Nice.
ko: 너의 청구는 사실상 존재하지 않아. 힘든 해였어. 너는 상담 요청을 무시해.

en: Look, Dr. House, the only reason why I don't fire you
is because your reputation is still worth something to this hospital.
ko: 나는 통로를 놓칠 때가 있어. 너는 이 클리닉에 대한 의무가 6년이나 뒤쳐져 있어.

en: Excellent. We have a point of agreement. You're not going to fire me.
Your reputation won't last if you don't do your job.
ko: 이건 나에게 흥미롭지 않아. 6년 곱하기 3주, 너는 나에게 4개월보다 더 잘해야 해.

en: The clinic is part of your job. I want you to do your job.
ko: 너의 일이니까 일을 잘 해줘. 철학자 재거가 한 말처럼, 항상 원하는 걸 얻을 수는 없어.

en: As the philosopher Jagger once said, you can't always get what you want.
ko: 철학자 재거가 말했듯이, 항상 원하는 걸 얻을 수는 없어.

en: You can't always get what you want.
ko: 항상 원하는 걸 얻을 수는 없어.
